name: Spatial Neighborhood Enrichment (Squidpy)
description: Calculate neighborhood enrichment between cluster categories using Squidpy. Identifies microenvironments by finding which cell types or spatial domains tend to co-locate or repel each other in the physical tissue space.

cluster_key.description: obs key containing discrete cluster/category labels

n_neighbors.description: Number of nearest neighbors to compute the spatial graph (used if radius is null)

radius.description: Distance radius to compute the spatial graph (overrides n_neighbors if provided)

n_perms.description: Number of permutations for computing the z-score

file.description: Spatial transcriptomics data (h5ad format) containing cluster/cell type labels in obs and spatial coordinates in obsm

output_config.collection_description: Outputs neighborhood enrichment z-scores indicating spatial co-localization or repulsion, along with visualization and data objects.

items.description: CSV file containing the neighborhood enrichment z-scores matrix. Rows and columns represent unique cluster labels from the provided cluster_key. Positive z-scores indicate spatial co-localization (enrichment) between the two cell types, while negative scores indicate spatial avoidance (depletion).
Heatmap image (PNG, 300 DPI) visualizing the neighborhood enrichment z-scores. Warmer colors indicate co-localization and cooler colors indicate avoidance.

AnnData object (h5ad format) storing the spatial neighborhood graph in `uns['spatial_neighbors']` and the computed enrichment metrics in `uns[cluster_key + '_nhood_enrichment']`. Useful for downstream Squidpy processing.
Text report summarizing the analysis parameters, number of clusters analyzed, spatial graph construction metrics, and execution time.
